posts.text: I've stopped visiting this neighborhood, but for those still around, I have a newsletter paulkrugman.substack.com/
For anyone interested, kind of fun event on decayed industry and what comes next. NOT about the election, except insofar as it affects regional policy 92ny.org/event/cathedrals-of…

Some analytical thoughts about the coming trade war

stonecenter.gc.cuny.edu/can-…
For the past few days it has felt as if nature was mocking us with beautiful weather following political disaster. But that seems to be over (wildfires in New Jersey!)

Don’t ask whose fault this was. Plenty of time for second-guessing and recriminations. Ask instead, what can you do? For my part, that means telling the truth as I see it, as long as I can. The media will be under a lot of pressure to toe the line; don’t capitulate in advance

If this goes the way it seems to be going, a plea: Hold the recriminations. The important thing will be figuring out how to fight the climate of repression, which will begin via preemptive obedience long before the government formally changes hands
Urban hellscape. People don’t dare leave their apartments
Our plan for today: Instead of sitting at home doomscrolling, we’re going to a museum, so we can doomscroll in front of great art

More on Harris closing the economy gap: partisan positions, especially R, largely immovable. So looking at improvement btwn Sept and Oct Times polls, it looks as if Harris had huge success in persuading the persuadable
Some worry that America may become a soft autocracy like Hungary, with one-party rule but not violent repression. We should be so lucky. nytimes.com/2024/11/04/opini…

One possibly important factor in the election: People are still a bit mad about high prices, but historical patterns suggest that most of this effect should have faded by now briefingbook.info/p/digestin…
I wonder how much Trump being the boy who cried “depression” has helped neutralize his once formidable advantage on the economy
Incredibly, people are still writing stories about how only the elite has benefited from the strong economy. In fact, the biggest gains have gone to low-wage workers

More on the incredible shrinking economy issue: at this point it looks like an election over abortion rights and immigration. And that Selzer poll suggests that we may be underestimating the POW — pissed-off women — vote

Polling suggests that Harris has more or less erased Trump’s advantage on the economy. Much of that is probably the reality of falling inflation. But a well-focused campaign has surely helped. nytimes.com/2024/11/04/us/po…
Trump says that armed migrants have taken over our cities. If so, they’re doing a remarkably good job of maintaining order nytimes.com/2024/11/02/upsho…
Never mind the prediction markets, which are thin and easily manipulated. Here’s the evidence for shifting vibes

Trump, overnight: “If Kamala wins, you are 3 days away from the start of a 1929-style economic depression.”

What has happened since the last time he predicted a depression if a Democrat won:

Hi all, Paul Krugman and I did a podcast on the Trump Administration and what might be happening in the bombing of Iran. Not the most uplifting of talks, but you might find it interesting. substack.com/@phillipspob...

Prof. @pkrugman.bsky.social and historian @hcrichardson.bsky.social joined forces today by launching "Lunch Money,” a new monthly conversation series about how money shapes our politics www.youtube.com/watch?v=e6vc...

Last night we welcomed Lina M. Khan, former FTC Chair, for an enlightening conversation with our own Paul Krugman, research professor in economics. Video of “Affordability, Antitrust, and Inequality” coming soon! Presented with @stone-lis.bsky.social (Photos: Matt Harvey)
Trump’s declaration that he expects UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER from Iran does not seem to be calming markets
Remember all those boasts about jobs and gasoline prices in the State of the Union? www.youtube.com/watch?v=P0Zv...

excellent deep dive into all of the ways that the Trump administration has used the federal budget to keep punishing critics and trying to bend them to his will.    FWIW, this is precisely how Viktor Orban crushed the opposition in Hungary 15 years ago.  The national budget is a powerful weapon.
Attacking Iran versus defending Ukraine www.youtube.com/watch?v=9Lng...

Many people talking about how gasoline prices at the pump have already jumped. But what should really terrify Republicans is RBOB — the futures price on wholesale gasoline. This is up 75 cents a gallon since its low earlier this year.

If affordability is a political issue, who’s really responsible?

Lina M. Khan and Paul Krugman discuss monopoly power, antitrust, and inequality at Affordability, Antitrust, and Inequality. @pkrugman.bsky.social 

March 9, 2026 | 6:30 PM

🔗 www.gc.cuny.edu/events/affor...

Join us!
Surprise: War in the middle of the world’s most important oil-producing region has consequences www.youtube.com/watch?v=55pk...
Trump just held a press conference about war with Iran, and spent much of it talking about drapes and his ballroom. I guess those are the drapes of wrath???
About Anthropic, Iran, and dumbing down www.youtube.com/watch?v=NjUo...
@pkrugman.bsky.social at his most trenchant. The Economics of Technological Change: What history and models can (and can’t) tell us about AI open.substack.com/pub/paulkrug...
Now that the Supremes have humiliated Trump, it’s time to invade the Falkland Islands, I mean, bomb Iran.
Larry Ellison is buying CNN. That’s a disaster www.youtube.com/watch?v=QEwy...
New video on why you won’t receive a tariff check www.youtube.com/watch?v=tEDA...
About growth in Europe and the US; it’s not the story you’ve heard open.substack.com/pub/paulkrug...
We’re pleased to announce a $10 million gift from the James M. and Cathleen D. Stone Foundation,  which supports the work of @stone-lis.bsky.social www.gc.cuny.edu/news/10-mill... @cuny.edu
Trump loves tariffs but the economy doesn't paulkrugman.substack.com/p/attack-of-...
What is a balance-of-payments deficit? I should know, but I don't. Nobody does. www.youtube.com/watch?v=VY6d...
